description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer who has been asked to gather a quick, self-contained audit proving that
  1) you can force every tool you run into UTC time,
  2) you can force an exact locale (“C”) for fully reproducible output, and
  3) you can capture a SHA-256 checksum of the data you just finished backing up.

  Starting situation:
    • Your home directory is /home/user  
    • A tiny data sample already exists at /home/user/sample_data.txt  
      – The file’s entire content is exactly three ASCII characters with **no** trailing line-feed:  
        abc  
    • There is **no** directory called /home/user/backup_logs yet.

  What needs to be done:
    1. Create the directory /home/user/backup_logs (if it does not yet exist).  
    2. In a **single one-off terminal session** set both of these environment variables *for that session only*:  
         TZ=UTC  
         LC_ALL=C  
       (Do **not** modify any global system files such as /etc/timezone or /etc/locale.*.)  
    3. Still inside the same session, produce an integrity-audit file called
         /home/user/backup_logs/integrity_check.log
       whose contents follow **exactly** the three-line, strict, plain-text format shown below (no extra spaces, no blank lines):

         TIMESTAMP:<iso-8601-utc-stamp>
         LOCALE:C
         CHECKSUM:ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

       Details for each line:
         • TIMESTAMP:<iso-8601-utc-stamp>  
           ‑ Must be produced by the command “date -u” (or equivalent) once you have set TZ=UTC.  
           ‑ Required format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ (24-hour, leading zeros, literal “T” and trailing “Z”).  
           ‑ Example of correct shape (your actual numbers will of course differ):  
             TIMESTAMP:2023-10-04T16:12:55Z  
         • LOCALE:C  
           ‑ Must literally be the single capital letter C; no quotes, no other wording.  
           ‑ Demonstrates that the session’s LC_ALL is in effect.  
         • CHECKSUM:<hash>  
           ‑ Must be the **full 64-character lowercase hexadecimal** sha256 sum of /home/user/sample_data.txt  
           ‑ Because the file content is strictly “abc” (no newline), the correct hash is  
              ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad  

  Additional verification requirements:
    • integrity_check.log must contain **exactly three** lines and nothing else.  
    • The directory /home/user/backup_logs and the log file itself must be world-readable (mode 644 for the file and 755 for the directory is fine).  
    • You must not use sudo or write to any file outside /home/user.  
    • All operations should be achievable with a very small set of standard GNU/Linux commands (around 3-4 commands total).  

  Once finished, double-check that:
     cat /home/user/backup_logs/integrity_check.log
  prints the three expected lines in the exact order and spelling described above.
truth:
Ground-truth objects that will be checked by the automated grader:

  1. /home/user/sample_data.txt  
     – File must already exist **before** the agent begins.  
     – Exact byte sequence (3 bytes, no trailing newline):  
       0x61 0x62 0x63   # “abc”

  2. Hash value that the grader will accept:  
       ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

  3. /home/user/backup_logs/integrity_check.log must exist at the end of the task.  
     – Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)  
     – Content must satisfy ALL of the following Boolean checks:

         a) File has exactly 3 newline-terminated text lines.  
         b) Line-1 matches regex  
              ^TIMESTAMP:[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$  
         c) Line-2 is literally the string  
              LOCALE:C  
         d) Line-3 is literally the string  
              CHECKSUM:ba7816bf8f01cfea414140de5dae2223b00361a396177a9cb410ff61f20015ad

  4. Directory /home/user/backup_logs must exist and be executable by “other” (mode 755 or more permissive).

  The grader will also spawn a subshell with “LC_ALL=C TZ=UTC” and independently recompute  
    sha256sum /home/user/sample_data.txt  
  to ensure the agent did not alter the sample file.
name: task_000000_494877cf
